guide.subtitle: Singapore · Participant Guide
guide.problemStatementsIntro: Each team picks one track only.
guide.track1Desc: This is the traditional hackathon impact track. Your project is scored on whether it could create real value in the market.
guide.track1Bullet1: The problem is real, and someone is willing to pay (or use it daily).
guide.track1Bullet2: The product is polished enough that the demo itself sells it.
guide.track1Bullet3: The craft, UX, and shipping-ness are visible from minute one.
guide.track1Bullet4: AI-native, but the AI is in service of a human user — not the other way around.
guide.track1Stack: Stack is free. Use Codex, use anything else. What you built matters more than what you built it with.
guide.track1Think: Think: AI-native SaaS, consumer apps, workflow automation, useful products people would actually pay for or open daily.
guide.track2Desc: Less about monetization, more about how well you tame and operate the agent. Like Oh My Codex, OpenClaw, gbrain, gstack, hermes — the harness wrapping the agent is itself the deliverable.
guide.track2Bullet1: The agent runs long, runs autonomously, and finishes its job without babysitting.
guide.track2Bullet2: Goal, termination, verification, and recovery are designed — not improvised.
guide.track2Bullet3: Multi-agent orchestration, tool use, memory, and loop structure are deliberate.
guide.track2Bullet4: A judge can read the harness and understand why it works, not just that it works.
guide.track2CodexRequired: Harness track must be integrated so it works with OpenAI Codex. MCP Server, Agents SDK, Direct API, /goal — integration method is free, but evaluation is anchored on Codex.
guide.track2Think: Think: autonomous coding agents, Codex-backed multi-agent systems, harnesses that wrap Codex with novel loop / memory / verification logic, meta-agents that improve themselves on Codex.
guide.trackTableRow1: Scored on|Business value · market fit · UX · polish|Agent design · autonomy · technical craft
guide.trackTableRow3: Why it's good|People want to use it|The agent is technically well-designed
guide.trackTableRow4: Keywords|useful, polished, shipping, AI-native|autonomous, long-running, agent-first, harness
guide.trackTableRow5: Reference|General AI products|Oh My Codex, OpenClaw, gbrain, gstack, hermes
guide.trackTableNote: Declare your track at submission. Prizes are awarded per track.
guide.notToDoIntro: Projects will be immediately disqualified if they fall into any of these categories:
guide.discordIntro: Join the Ralphthon Discord for team formation, announcements, and Q&A:
guide.rule1: Team Size: Maximum 4 members per team. Solo participants are welcome.
guide.rule2: Track Lock-in: One team, one track. Declare at submission.
guide.rule3: Open Source: Submitted repositories must be public.
guide.rule4: New Work Only: You may not present existing projects as your own work. Judges will check.
guide.rule5: Demo Requirements: Your demo must highlight only features built during the hackathon. Failure to clearly identify original contributions = disqualification.
guide.rule6: Harness Track = Codex integration required: Harness submissions that don't integrate with Codex are auto-disqualified.
guide.rule7: Banned Projects: Projects will be disqualified if they violate legal, ethical, or platform policies, or use code/data/assets you don't have rights to.
guide.mediaNoticeText: This hackathon is being filmed by a professional video crew. If you prefer not to appear on camera, please let us know at check-in — we'll provide a sticker for your name badge so the crew knows to keep you out of frame.
guide.judgingDesc: Judges walk the floor and visit each team's table. ~3 minutes live demo + 1-2 minutes Q&A per team.
guide.judgingNote: Show us what you have built. Please do not show us a presentation. We'll be checking to ensure your project was built entirely during the event; no previous work is allowed.
guide.judgingScorecardDesc: One card per judge per team.
guide.judgingScoreHeader: Team name|Judge name|Live demo (0-4)|Creativity / Originality (0-3)|Impact potential (0-3)
guide.judgingC1: Live Demo (0-4)|How well does the team implement the core idea? Does it actually work live?
guide.judgingC2: Creativity / Originality (0-3)|Has this been seen before? Does it tackle the track's problem in an original way?
guide.judgingC3: Impact Potential (0-3)|Will this survive past the hackathon? How useful is it?
guide.judgingTracks: Impact and Harness are awarded separately. The same scorecard is used, but for Harness, 'Live demo' evaluation includes Codex integration quality.
guide.contact: Questions? Reach out to GB Jeong — bong@team-attention.com
